channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: How Modi’s AI handholding moment backfired as Sam and Dario refused to play along
item.description: “I didn't know what was happening,” Altman later said, adding he was “confused, like when (Modi) grabbed my hand and put it up."

item.title: Trump orders government to release files on 'extremely important' matter of aliens, UFOs
item.description: President Trump says he is ordering the government to release documents related to aliens and UFOs.

item.title: FBI chief Kash Patel jets off to Italy on a government plane in time to watch Olympic hockey games
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel took a Justice Department plane to Italy, where he is expected to attend a Winter Olympics hockey event.

item.title: The only map you need to see to understand how serious Trump is about Iran
item.description: U.S. military buildup in Persian Gulf intensifies as Trump deploys naval force near Iran. Aircraft carriers and destroyers position for potential stri...

item.title: GDP growth sharply slows to 1.4%, less than half of fourth quarter expectations
item.description: Economists say the small bump in unemployment rates is due to the Trump Administration's immigration crackdown stalling population growth.

item.title: First, Colbert, now ‘The View’: Trump’s FCC opens equal time investigation into political chat show
item.description: “The FCC has an enforcement action underway on that,” Chairman Brendan Carr told reporters after an agency meeting Wednesday.

item.title: Democrats Claim GOP ‘Gutted’ Medicaid. Federal Data Shows The Opposite
item.description: The latest CBO report illustrates how Medicaid spending, like that of other federal health care programs, continues to grow ever higher.

item.title: Collateral Damage for the Coal Industry?
item.description: A lawsuit over ESG policies could have unintended consequences.

item.title: Something big is happening in AI, but that’s the only thing Matt Shumer got right
item.description: This could be the greatest democratizing event of all time.

item.title: Cartel and Chinese Drones Demand Immediate FAA Action
item.description: The Border Patrol and the military need clear authority to deploy counter-drone systems in sensitive areas without endless FAA vetoes.

item.title: How Developers Are Making AI Your Kid’s Third Parent In The Classroom
item.description: The CEOs of Anthropic and OpenAI admit AI is like a parent nobody can resist, while teachers unions support Big Tech’s rule.

item.title: Supreme Court blocks Trump tariffs in major test of executive branch powers
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled on one of the president's signature economic achievements, which Trump has billed the tariff case as 'life or death' for the U...

item.title: This math graduate applied to over 1,000 jobs and still didn’t hear back—so he moved to Austria
item.description: Gen Z and millennial workers in Britain are so fed up with being ghosted by employers that some are giving up on the country altogether.

item.title: Unmasking The Muslim Brotherhood Ties Inside Ohio’s General Assembly
item.description: In a highly anticipated move, the Trump administration designated factions of the global Muslim Brotherhood as terrorist organizations last month, an ...

item.title: The Bogus Americana of Train Dreams
item.description: Bentley, feeling superior to the past, falls short of Malick’s visual poetry.

item.title: From slave roots to Senate race: Allen West bets on Wesley Hunt's Cinderella story
item.description: If you're looking for a Cinderella story in the Texas GOP Senate race, retired Lt. Col. Allen West thinks former Apache helicopter pilot Wesley Hunt m...

item.title: Trump says he is 'considering' a limited military strike to pressure Iran into nuclear deal
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday he is "considering" a limited military strike on Iran to pressure its leaders into a deal over its nuclear program.

item.title: A third of GOP voters warn they may sit out midterms if party ‘abandons’ pro-life fight, poll finds
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is facing criticism from pro-life advocates over mail-order abortion drug policies, with 80% saying they suppor...

item.title: Media Mock Gen Z For Rotisserie Chickens Because It’s Easier Than Addressing Real Problems
item.description: It's easier to highlight the spending habits of twenty-somethings than to interrogate the policy decisions that shaped today’s cost of living.

item.title: What Would George Washington Do?
item.description: Adhering to Washington’s ‘Rules of Civility’ would probably solve a good deal of our cultural problems.

item.title: Religious Freedom Is Syria’s First Test
item.description: Syria’s future depends on whether it will protect its most vulnerable citizens — Christians and Kurds.

item.title: North Korea opens major party congress with Kim emphasizing economy
item.description: North Korea has opened its most important political event in which leader Kim Jong Un is expected to map out his domestic and foreign policy agenda fo...

item.title: Democratic-socialist nominee eyeing NJ governor's vacant House seat compares ICE to 1960s segregationists
item.description: Analilia Mejia invoked the Birmingham Children’s Crusade while railing against federal immigration efforts following her victory in the New Jersey pri...

item.title: Why Static Portfolios Fail When Risk Regimes Change
item.description: Why static portfolio frameworks fail when risk regimes shift, drawing lessons from the very different market breakdowns of 2020 and 2022. The post Why...

item.title: The Week: Mr. Rubio Goes to Munich
item.description: Plus: Zohran Mamdani’s budget blunder.

item.title: Large banner featuring Trump's face is displayed on Justice Department headquarters
item.description: A large banner featuring Donald Trump's face was hung on the exterior of Justice Department headquarters on Thursday in a physical display of the Repu...

item.title: What the Market Knows That WACC Doesn’t
item.description: Market-implied discount rates reveal how investors price risk, often diverging from WACC and reshaping capital decisions. The post What the Market Kno...

item.title: Attention Bias in AI-Driven Investing
item.description: AI tools may favor popular stocks over overlooked ones, embedding attention bias into investment decisions. The post Attention Bias in AI-Driven Inves...

item.title: Social Security Claiming Strategies for High-Net-Worth Clients
item.description: Social Security claiming is a capital-allocation decision for affluent clients. This analysis weighs taxes, longevity risk, and liquidity trade-offs. ...

item.title: Evolving Your Wealth Management Practice for 2026 and Beyond
item.description: Wealth management’s 2026 outlook: growth will hinge on transparency, integration, and relevance to women and next-generation investors. The post Evolv...
